author: @benjaminn8
summary: orders list no longer exposes --sort-key or --reverse; the v3.2.1 printing carried them via a now-superseded query/search/resolve patch.
reason: v3.2.1 (printed by @cathrynlavery) carried a `orders-query-search-resolve` patch that wired --sort-key, --reverse, and several orders search helpers into the generated orders surface. This reprint replaces that approach with the generic --query flag wiring (patch list-query-flag-wiring), which covers the same filter use cases via Shopify's native search syntax and does not need bespoke sort flags. Automations relying on the old --sort-key / --reverse will see unknown-flag errors and must switch to --query 'sort_key:created_at-asc' or equivalent search-syntax expressions. Recording this here so future regen and reprint tooling treats the removal as intentional, not a regression.
validated_outcome: shopify-pp-cli orders list --help no longer advertises --sort-key/--reverse; --query covers the same filter shapes.
